page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8y.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores on the custom 64-bit ARM architecture, utilizing the Alibaba YiTian710 processor from the Yitian family. Operating at a clock speed of 2.75 GHz, each core is single-threaded and has access to 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and a shared 64 MB L3 cache. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the ecs.g8y.xlarge in the average performance tier for most standard operations. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng yield average results, with scores of 1819.78 and 7230.46 respectively. Memory subsystem performance is mixed; while cached small-block read bandwidth is average at 407.26 GB/sec, large-block uncached write bandwidth is weak at 25.44 GB/sec. Cryptographic performance is a notable bottleneck, with the PassMark CPU encryption test scoring in the poor tier at 659.83, despite hardware support for AES instructions. However, the instance demonstrates solid capabilities in database operations and web serving, achieving an average of 6.71 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a static web serving throughput of 6.51 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based YiTian710 architecture offers a resource-dense profile for general-purpose workloads that do not require x86 instruction sets or specialized vector extensions. The absence of local storage means users must rely on network-attached storage, which should be factored into the overall architectural design. Given its balanced CPU-to-memory ratio and average performance in database and web serving benchmarks, this instance is well-suited for applications such as static web serving, Redis caching layers, lightweight database hosting, and general-purpose microservices. It is less suitable for heavy cryptographic tasks or large-scale LLM inference due to poor benchmark results in those areas.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8y.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by the 2.75 GHz Alibaba YiTian710 processor. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, a 64 MB L3 cache, and a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmark data indicates average performance for general compute, database operations, and web serving, highlighted by 6.71 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a static web serving throughput of 6.51 GB/sec. However, write memory bandwidth and cryptographic execution are weaker, with PassMark encryption scoring in the bottom decile. This instance offers a qualitatively cost-efficient ARM profile for balanced workloads, making it suitable for web serving, caching, and lightweight database applications.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8y.xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 4 dedicated YiTian710 cores, 16 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for web serving and caching.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8y.xlarge features 4 ARM64 YiTian710 cores and 16 GB RAM, delivering balanced performance for web serving and database workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered general purpose instance delivering balanced compute and memory for web serving and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 4 dedicated ARM64 Alibaba YiTian710 cores running at 2.75 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory and a 64 MB L3 cache
Delivers 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no bundled local storage
Achieves 6.71 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Best suited for static web serving, caching, and lightweight database hosting
